texto: Fate has dug me a hole, and rather than crawling out, I’m digging it deeper. What Fate began with a post-hole digger, I have expanded with a backhoe. I think I expect that when I reach bottom, I’ll find some sort of enlightenment - that which would give my life meaning, like a buried treasure. It may be buried treasure, but I think it’s buried deep within my soul. It may even be shouting to be let out.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a P.J. Paulson: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de sentido da existência e experiência vivida. O trecho “Fate has dug me a hole, and rather than crawling out, I’m digging it deeper. What Fate began with…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, fate, vida pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de vida e meaning.
